heading: What is electromagnetic induction?
definition: Electromagnetic induction is the process of generating an electromotive force (EMF) and consequently an electric current in a closed circuit by changing the magnetic flux passing through it.

concepts.title: Faraday's Law
concepts.description: The induced EMF is proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux linkage.

concepts.title: Lenz's Law
concepts.description: Direction of induced current opposes the change that caused it.

concepts.title: Fleming's Right Hand Rule
concepts.description: Determines direction of induced current in a moving conductor.

variableDefs:
EMF = electromotive force (Volts)
N = number of turns in the coil
ΔΦ = change in magnetic flux (Webers)
Δt = time interval (seconds)
